methodsAttempted.query: site:um.es inteligencia artificial generativa ChatGPT normativa estudiantes universidad de murcia
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search surfaced the official University of Murcia library AI page, a 2023 ATICA ChatGPT event page, a tv.um.es event video page, and research/publication records that were not policy evidence.

methodsAttempted.query: site:um.es "IA generativa" "Universidad de Murcia" estudiantes evaluación
methodsAttempted.notes: Additional Spanish-language searches found academic journal/course pages and official news/event coverage, but no formal university-wide AI policy or assessment regulation page.
The verified evidence source is hosted on www.um.es under the official Biblioteca site. robots.txt allows the path and sets Crawl-delay: 1.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery located a substantive University of Murcia library page titled "IA en la biblioteca". The search did not locate a formal university-wide AI policy; event/news pages and research records were retained only as rejected context.

artifacts.verificationNotes: Official University of Murcia library page on www.um.es with AI classroom recommendations, AI citation guidance, tool categories, and links to external AI guidance resources. Treated as official library guidance, not a formal university-wide AI regulation.

artifacts.sourceTitle: El futuro de la educación superior: ChatGPT y la Inteligencia Artificial en la mira de la Universidad de Murcia
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official ATICA news page about a 2023 reflection event. It discusses expert views and possible future regulation, but it is not a policy, rule, or operational guidance source.

artifacts.sourceTitle: La Universidad de Murcia aborda desde todas las perspectivas el impacto del ChatGPT y la Inteligencia Artificial en la enseñanza superior
artifacts.queryUsed: site:um.es "IA generativa" "Universidad de Murcia" estudiantes evaluación
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official press release about a 2023 ChatGPT/AI event. It records discussion themes but does not itself set AI policy or assessment rules.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Escritura, creatividad e inteligencia artificial. ChatGPT en el contexto universitario
artifacts.verificationNotes: Institutional research portal record for scholarship involving ChatGPT; not official university policy or guidance.

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: La página describe una jornada dedicada a explorar los usos de la IA y ChatGPT en la educación universitaria, no una norma o guía operativa.
artifacts.notes: Not used for claims because event summaries and quotes should not be promoted into binding University of Murcia AI policy.

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: La nota de prensa dice que la jornada abordó el impacto de ChatGPT y la IA en la enseñanza superior.
artifacts.notes: Official but not used as policy evidence because it is a press summary of a discussion event.

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: Search discovery identified it as a research document record, not an institutional rule or guidance document.
artifacts.notes: Institutional repository or research portal scholarship may inform context but is not policy evidence for this tracker.

stopConditions: Use only official University of Murcia sources as claim evidence.
Do not promote event pages, research portal records, or external guidance links into University of Murcia policy claims.
Preserve Spanish evidence snippets as canonical.

artifacts.claimText: The University of Murcia library AI page advises that, for TFG/TFM work, instructors should tell students they need instructor confirmation before using AI tools.

citation.publisher: Biblioteca de la Universidad de Murcia
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: The University of Murcia library AI page gives AI citation guidance, including describing AI use in methodology, including the prompt and relevant response fragment, citing reliable sources, and checking the citation style.

citation.publisher: Biblioteca de la Universidad de Murcia

artifacts.claimText: The University of Murcia library AI page summarizes European research guidance saying researchers should abstain from using generative AI tools in sensitive activities such as peer review or evaluations, and should use generative AI respecting privacy, confidentiality, and intellectual property rights.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Aprobación docente. El docente debe informar al alumno que para la realización de trabajos (TFG/TFM) necesita la confirmación del docente antes de usar herramientas de IA.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Instructor approval. The instructor should inform the student that, for TFG/TFM work, instructor confirmation is needed before using AI tools.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Pautas: Descripción en Metodología. Hay que describir como se han empleado en el apartado de metodología del trabajo. Entrada y Respuesta. artifacts.notes: Approved as narrow library guidance, not a university-wide AI prohibition or formal academic integrity rule.
Approved as official library citation guidance with Spanish evidence retained.
Approved as a responsible-use/privacy framing from the official library page.
Approved with wording that this is the University library page summarizing European research guidance, not an independently adopted UMU research policy.
Approved as source-status only because the page lists tool categories but does not identify approved or prohibited AI tools.

artifacts.title: University of Murcia AI Library Guidance Source Review
artifacts.summary: Official discovery for University of Murcia found an AI-specific library page with classroom, citation, responsible-use, research, and AI tool-listing guidance. No formal university-wide AI policy page was located in the official-domain search pass; event and research-portal pages were rejected as non-policy context.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The verified source is an official library guidance page, not a formal university-wide AI regulation.
Event/news pages and research portal records were not used as claim evidence.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
